selected_goal: Stop legacy mark-scheme answer blocks before a missed neighboring question row on the same page.
why_this_now: The user specifically highlighted one mark scheme image including multiple questions. Legacy mark schemes can miss a neighboring anchor when the row layout differs slightly, causing the previous question crop to include later answer rows.
repo_evidence: src/exam_bank/mark_schemes.py has legacy anchor and bounds logic for historical answer-table layouts.
The previous boundary logic depends mostly on detected anchors, so a missed neighbor can leave the crop bottom too low.
tests/test_question_detection.py contains mark-scheme parser tests suitable for synthetic legacy block layouts.
acceptance_criteria: A legacy mark-scheme block stops before a later top-level question row even when that row is not part of the primary anchor sequence.
The rendered mark-scheme region bottom is clamped to the computed foreign boundary.
test_expectations: Add a synthetic legacy test with a missed later neighbor row.
Confirm the test fails before implementation.
Run the focused test and the full question-detection test module after implementation.
implementation_boundaries: Do not loosen anchor numbering enough to classify notes pages as answer rows.
Do not modify OCR or generated output.
Keep the boundary logic limited to legacy mark-scheme processing.
repo_hygiene_risk: Low to medium: production mark-scheme bounds logic changes, but the edit is local and covered by a focused regression.
rollback_plan: Remove the legacy foreign-boundary helper and the associated regression test.
done_when: The missed-neighbor legacy mark-scheme regression passes.
No generated output files are modified.
